rationale.R1.5: The output provides detailed suggestions for reorganizing the abstract to improve clarity and coherence without proposing a new end-to-end section plan.
rationale.RX.1: The feedback flags several areas of uncertainty in the claims made, such as the gap-closing claim and the statistical significance of results.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) without any drift or additional scope.
rationale.RX.4: The feedback does not introduce new errors, fabrications, or harmful content but points out potential downstream harms such as overclaiming and strong deployment claims.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, though the model identity of the judge is not explicitly stated.
